ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "account_name" TEXT /* Username of the Twitter account */,
  "account_created_date" TEXT /* Date the account was created */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "account_location" TEXT /* Location specified in the user's profile */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language used by the user */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the account is verified */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "description" TEXT /* User's bio or description */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the user's website */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Last time the user was active */,
  "account_activated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the account is activated */,
  "account_suspended" INTEGER /* Indicates if the account is suspended */,
  "account_suspended_reason" TEXT /* Reason for account suspension */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "tweet_date" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was posted */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "favorite_count" INTEGER /* Number of favorites (likes) the tweet received */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet received */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "in_reply_to_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet this tweet is replying to */,
  "in_reply_to_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this tweet is replying to */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter for iPhone) */,
  "is_retweeted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "retweet_of_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet if this tweet is a retweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from where the tweet was posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_in_reply_to_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("in_reply_to_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_in_reply_to_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("in_reply_to_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_retweet_of_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("retweet_of_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being analyzed */,
  "compound_score" REAL /* Compound sentiment score */,
  "positive_score" REAL /* Positive sentiment score */,
  "negative_score" REAL /* Negative sentiment score */,
  "neutral_score" REAL /* Neutral sentiment score */,
  "sentiment_confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the sentiment analysis */,
  "subjectivity_score" REAL /* Subjectivity score of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_keywords" TEXT /* Key words or phrases that influenced the sentiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_analysis_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being engaged with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user engaging with the tweet */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., like, retweet) */,
  "engagement_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the engagement */,
  "reply_text" TEXT /* Text of the reply if the engagement is a reply */,
  "reply_to_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being replied to */,
  "reply_to_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being replied to */,
  "engagement_confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the engagement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_reply_to_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("reply_to_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_reply_to_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("reply_to_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "trend_date" TEXT /* Date and time the trend was observed */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of trend (e.g., hashtag, keyword) */,
  "trend_value" TEXT /* Value of the trend (e.g., #Bitcoin, #LightningNetwork) */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the trend */,
  "trend_volume" INTEGER /* Volume of the trend (e.g., number of tweets, mentions) */,
  "trend_sentiment" TEXT /* Overall sentiment of the trend (positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "trend_reach" INTEGER /* Reach of the trend (e.g., number of unique users involved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trends_trend_type FOREIGN KEY ("trend_type") REFERENCES tweets ("text")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language used by the user */,
  "interests" TEXT /* User's interests */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* User's education level */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* User's occupation */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* User's income range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_demographics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_created_date" TEXT /* Date the role was created */,
  "role_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the role was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table the data version belongs to */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record being versioned */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date and time the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version (e.g., initial version, update) */,
  "version_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "version_reason" TEXT /* Reason for creating the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_version_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
